rag.description: ClearElementContent removes text from input elements during automation. It supports a configurable delay before clearing via the Delay parameter and a NativeClear switch to handle elements that resist the standard WebDriver clear command by simulating backspace key presses.

qa.question: What is the primary purpose of the ClearElementContent action?
qa.answer: It removes all existing text or values from a specified input or textarea element to ensure a clean state for subsequent automation steps.

qa.question: Which parameters can be used to customize the clearing process?
qa.answer: The action supports a Delay parameter for synchronization and a NativeClear switch to enable keyboard-simulated clearing for complex elements.

qa.question: What locator strategies does ClearElementContent support?
qa.answer: It supports any G4-supported locator strategy via the Locator property, including Xpath (default), CssSelector, Id, LinkText, and PartialLinkText.

qa.question: When should I use the NativeClear option?
qa.answer: Use NativeClear when an element has custom event listeners or input masks that prevent the standard browser clear method from working correctly. It simulates repeated backspace key presses to remove content.

qa.question: How does the Delay parameter work?
qa.answer: Delay specifies a wait time before the clear operation begins, accepted as a TimeSpan string such as 00:00:02 for two seconds. It is useful for dynamic elements that load or update asynchronously before clearing.

qa.question: What rule type is used for this plugin in a G4 manifest?
qa.answer: This plugin uses the Action rule type with the pluginName set to ClearElementContent.

qa.question: What is the namespace and plugin type for ClearElementContent?
qa.answer: The namespace is G4.Plugins.Ui.Actions and the pluginType is Action.

qa.question: How does the ClearElementContent action improve automation reliability?
qa.answer: It ensures that input fields are cleared of any stale or pre-filled data, preventing errors that occur when new text is appended to existing values.

qa.question: What happens if the target element for the ClearElementContent action is not found?
qa.answer: The action logs a NoSuchElementException in the plugin response and terminates the clear operation for that specific rule.

qa.question: What is the manifest version for ClearElementContent?

description: ### Purpose
Removes all text and values from a targeted web element, ensuring subsequent interactions start from a clean state.
It prevents errors caused by pre-filled data and provides a native clearing option for elements that do not respond to the standard WebDriver clear method.
### Key Features and Functionality
| Feature        | Description                                                                                |
| Standard Clear | Uses the built-in browser clear method to remove element content.                          |
| Native Clear   | Simulates backspace key presses to clear elements that do not respond to standard methods. |
| Delay Support  | Allows specifying a wait time before the clear operation occurs.                           |
### Usages in RPA
| Use Case           | Description                                                                   |
| Form Reset         | Clearing all input fields in a web form before entering new transaction data. |
| Search Box Cleanup | Removing previous search terms before initiating a new lookup.                |
### Usages in Automation Testing
| Use Case         | Description                                                                  |
| Input Validation | Ensuring a field is empty before testing mandatory field constraints.        |
| Data Integrity   | Clearing persistent user profile fields before updating them with test data. |

edge_cases: Autocomplete suggestions reappear after clearing
Element covered by an overlay or modal
Element has pre-filled data restored by JavaScript
Element inside a shadow DOM boundary
Element inside an iframe
Element is disabled
Element is hidden or not displayed
Element is read-only
Element not yet rendered in the DOM
Target element not found
annotations.expected_result: The text content of the specified input field is completely removed.
annotations.notes: The action uses the standard browser clear method to reset the form field state. No additional parameters are needed for basic clearing.

description: ### Reset form field content
Clears all existing text from the target input element located by a CSS selector.
No additional parameters are passed, so the standard browser clear method is used.
Use this form for straightforward input fields that respond correctly to the WebDriver clear command.

edge_cases: Complex event listeners blocking standard clear
Content-editable element contains formatted HTML
Element inside a custom web component
Element text length exceeds viewport height
Focus lost during backspace key sequence
Input masking or custom formatters interfering
JavaScript re-populates content during clearing
No caret position available in the target element
Shadow DOM boundary prevents direct interaction
Standard clear silently fails without error
annotations.expected_result: The element content is cleared by simulating backspace key presses.
annotations.notes: Native clearing sends repeated backspace key events to the element. It is useful for content-editable divs and elements with custom input handlers that ignore the standard WebDriver clear command.

description: ### Clear element using native keyboard events
The `NativeClear` parameter simulates physical keyboard interactions to clear elements that resist the standard browser clear method.
The action sends backspace commands to the content-editable element until all text is removed, bypassing standard clearing limitations.

edge_cases: Animation still in progress when clear begins
Asynchronous data loading replaces content after clear
Delay exceeds session or implicit wait timeout
Element removed from DOM during delay period
Element state changes between delay and clear
Network latency affects element readiness
Page navigation occurs during delay
Search suggestions overlay blocking the element
Wait timeout exceeded before element becomes interactive
annotations.expected_result: The action waits for two seconds before clearing the search input field.
annotations.notes: The Delay parameter accepts a TimeSpan value in HH:mm:ss format. Adding a delay improves reliability when the target element appears or updates dynamically after page load.

description: ### Clear search box with delay
The rule applies a two-second delay before performing the clear operation on the search input.
The `Delay` parameter is set to `00:00:02` using the `{{$ ...}}` macro format to pass the value at runtime.
It helps synchronize the automation with the application state, ensuring the element is ready to be cleared.

edge_cases: Browser autofill restores value after clear
Client-side validation differs from server-side
Element triggers validation before clear completes
Element validation triggered on blur event
Field has placeholder text confused with actual value
Mandatory field indicator does not update after clear
Multiple validation rules on the same field
Read-only field prevents clearing
Validation error message not dismissed after re-entry
annotations.expected_result: The mandatory input field is cleared to allow for validation testing.
annotations.notes: Clearing a field is a common step when testing that an application correctly handles empty mandatory inputs. The CSS selector targets the element by its ID attribute.

description: ### Clear field for input validation
The action clears a mandatory email field to prepare for a validation check.
The element is located using a CSS selector targeting the `#user-email` ID.
It ensures that subsequent steps can verify if the application correctly identifies the missing required data.
Delay specifies the amount of time to wait before the clear operation begins.
It helps ensure that any previous page updates or animations are finished before the content is removed.
Using a delay can improve reliability when interacting with dynamic elements that load asynchronously.
parameters.name: Delay

description: NativeClear determines whether to use simulated keyboard events to clear the element content.
It matters because some elements may not respond to standard clearing methods due to custom scripts or event listeners.
Enabling this option ensures that the element is thoroughly cleared even in complex scenarios.
parameters.name: NativeClear

description: Argument provides the CLI-formatted parameter string for the clearing operation.
Use the macro syntax `{{$ --NativeClear}}` to trigger native backspace-based clearing or `{{$ --Delay:00:00:02}}` to set a wait before the clear.
Both parameters can be combined in a single argument such as `{{$ --NativeClear --Delay:00:00:02}}`.
properties.name: Argument

description: Locator specifies the strategy used to find the targeted element for clearing.
It determines how the automation engine identifies the element on the page.
properties.name: Locator

description: OnElement defines the unique identifier for the element that will have its content removed.
It points the action to the exact location where the clear operation should occur.
properties.name: OnElement

summary: ClearElementContent removes all text and values from a targeted web element, ensuring subsequent data entry starts from a clean state.
It supports a configurable delay before clearing and a native keyboard-simulation mode for elements that resist the standard WebDriver clear method.
Common uses include form field reset in RPA workflows and empty-state validation in automated test suites.
